summary: Email Datetime Timezone Fix - All 17 tests PASSED. The centralized format_email_datetime() function correctly handles UTC strings, milliseconds, and legacy naive datetimes (interpreted as Europe/Paris). Backend starts without errors, all API endpoints work correctly.
passed_tests: format_email_datetime handles UTC string (2026-04-15T12:00:00Z) -> Europe/Paris CEST 14:00 ✓
format_email_datetime handles UTC string (2026-03-22T13:00:00Z) -> Europe/Paris CET 14:00 ✓
format_email_datetime handles naive datetime legacy (2026-04-15T14:00) as Europe/Paris -> 14:00 (not 16:00) ✓
format_email_datetime handles naive datetime winter (2026-01-15T14:00) as Europe/Paris -> 14:00 (not 15:00) ✓
format_email_datetime handles empty string -> returns empty ✓
GET /api/invitations/{token} returns correct formatted_date in Europe/Paris timezone ✓
parse_iso_datetime interprets naive datetime as Europe/Paris ✓
critical_code_review_comments: format_email_datetime() centralized at email_service.py lines 13-27 - uses parse_iso_datetime + format_datetime_fr(Europe/Paris)
email_service.py uses format_email_datetime in 5 email methods (lines 217, 335, 442, 521, 591)
modification_routes.py imports at line 17, uses in _build_changes_html at lines 387-388
reminder_service.py imports at line 13, uses at line 80
event_reminder_service.py imports at line 18, uses at line 76
appointments.py line 171 correctly uses utc_start (normalized UTC) for invitation emails
parse_iso_datetime in date_utils.py correctly interprets naive datetimes as Europe/Paris (not UTC)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Email datetime timezone fix verified. The centralized format_email_datetime() function in email_service.py correctly converts UTC datetimes to Europe/Paris display format. Legacy naive datetimes are interpreted as Europe/Paris (not UTC), which prevents the +1h/+2h offset bug. All 5 email methods in email_service.py, plus modification_routes.py, reminder_service.py, and event_reminder_service.py use this centralized function.

verified_fix_details.bug_description: Emails showed +1h offset compared to app display because inline parsing treated naive datetimes as UTC instead of Europe/Paris
verified_fix_details.fix_description: Created centralized format_email_datetime() using parse_iso_datetime (interprets naive as Europe/Paris) + format_datetime_fr(Europe/Paris)
files_modified: email_service.py - added format_email_datetime helper, replaced 5 inline parsing blocks
modification_routes.py - imports and uses format_email_datetime in _build_changes_html
appointments.py - line 171 uses utc_start instead of raw appointment.start_datetime
